title: rhinanthus asperulus var. rohlenae
intro: Rhinanthus asperulus var. rohlenae, also known as Rohlena's orache, is a plant species within the orach family and is a parasitic plant that grows on the roots of other plants. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant effects, which help to alleviate coughs and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Rohlena's orache include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and act as a natural diuretic. Rohlena's orache is typically prepared as a tea or infusion, and it can also be dried and used in herbal remedies, such as tinctures and salves.
